company_types: Commercial real estate operators with complex facilities
icp.size: 1,000+ employees, multi-facility operations, significant maintenance budgets
use_cases: 3D digital twin scanning of complex facilities
Knowledge transfer from retiring veteran employees
Site-specific training for new hires using digital replicas
pain_points: Veteran employees retiring with decades of facility knowledge — no way to capture it
Maintenance teams can't locate assets or visualize work orders in context
Training new hires on complex facilities is slow and inconsistent
No single source of truth for facility layout, assets, and SOPs
Outage planning and facility upgrades rely on outdated drawings and tribal knowledge
buying_triggers: Wave of retirements losing institutional knowledge
Major facility outage or maintenance failure
New plant manager or VP of Operations hired
Facility expansion or acquisition requiring documentation
Safety incident linked to poor asset visibility or training gaps
Digital transformation initiative from executive leadership

accounts.name: DuPont de Nemours
accounts.why_fit: 32 manufacturing plants, 40% workforce retiring in 5 years, critical SOP documentation gaps.

accounts.name: NextEra Energy
accounts.why_fit: 150+ power generation facilities, aging asset inventory, major outage risk from knowledge loss.

accounts.name: Valero Energy
accounts.why_fit: 15 refineries, complex legacy systems, safety-critical maintenance requiring digital documentation.

accounts.name: Sealed Air Corporation
accounts.why_fit: 110+ production facilities worldwide, high turnover in maintenance, needs rapid onboarding system.

accounts.name: Holcim Ltd
accounts.why_fit: 70+ cement plants, aging infrastructure, multi-site asset tracking nightmare across regions.

accounts.name: Crown Holdings
accounts.why_fit: 150+ plants, complex machinery documentation, training consistency issue for new hires.

accounts.name: Brookfield Infrastructure
accounts.why_fit: 500+ commercial facilities, recent acquisitions need rapid asset documentation and integration.

accounts.name: Eastman Chemical
accounts.why_fit: 14 major production sites, technical expertise loss from retirements, SOP capture urgency.

accounts.name: Xylem Inc
accounts.why_fit: 40+ facilities, water & wastewater systems need maintenance visibility and knowledge transfer.

accounts.name: Flowserve Corporation
accounts.why_fit: 50+ global plants, complex pump/valve manufacturing needs digital maintenance work order integration.

outreach.email_subject: Wilmington safety gap before OSHA audit
outreach.context: Chemical manufacturers with 20+ sites capture SOPs before compliance audits hit.
outreach.problem: 32-site documentation gap exposed at Wilmington. OSHA deadline Q2 — plant managers need unified SOP capture now.
outreach.question: How are you documenting critical procedures across all sites today?
outreach.linkedin_connect: Saw the Wilmington safety incident. Most plants in your position use centralized SOP capture before OSHA audits close. Worth a quick call?

first_30_days: Call DuPont Wilmington plant manager Monday morning, reference OSHA audit deadline.
Email NextEra compliance officer Friday with FERC timeline context, request call.
LinkedIn message Valero VP Ops: EPA letter, 90-day compliance window.
Phone Sealed Air facilities director, mention training backlog and new hire failures.
Research Holcim regional ops contacts, prepare asset tracking audit data.
Call Crown Holdings plant manager, lead with quality incident corrective action.
Email Brookfield integration lead, offer asset documentation for acquisition completion.
Phone Eastman Chemical Kingsport site lead, emphasize retirement wave timeline.
LinkedIn Xylem maintenance director, reference wastewater contract renewal risk.
Call Flowserve quality lead, tie procedure documentation to manufacturing defect root cause.

sequencing.why: Active compliance deadlines and safety incidents create immediate budget authority and 30-60 day buying cycles.

channels.reasoning: Compliance deadlines demand real-time conversation; email alone gets buried in legal/ops queues.

metrics: Calls completed daily per account list
Qualified conversations with maintenance VPs or plant managers
Meetings booked with budget owners in days 8-15
Pipeline value from accounts with active compliance triggers
risks: Legal/compliance teams may gate executive access until audit closes.
Facility managers fear product pilots delay compliance remediation timelines.
Acquisition integrations pause all non-critical vendor conversations mid-quarter.
